# FHIR Queries: Familial Hypercholesterolemia Population Identification

---

## GROUP 1 — FH Diagnoses (Condition)

> Targets ICD-10-CM E78.01 (the specific FH code, distinct from generic hyperlipidemia) plus the SNOMED parent and heterozygous/homozygous sub-concepts. `clinical-status=active` excludes resolved/entered-in-error records.

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|267434003,http://snomed.info/sct|373423003,http://snomed.info/sct|373422008,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E78.01&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

| Code | System | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `267434003` | SNOMED CT | Familial hypercholesterolaemia (parent concept) |
| `373423003` | SNOMED CT | Heterozygous familial hypercholesterolaemia |
| `373422008` | SNOMED CT | Homozygous familial hypercholesterolaemia |
| `E78.01` | ICD-10-CM | Familial hypercholesterolemia |

> ⚠️ **Do NOT use E78.00** (pure hypercholesterolemia, unspecified) — this is a non-specific code that would massively overcount. E78.01 is the coded FH-specific entry. If your EHR predates ICD-10 conversion, legacy ICD-9 code `272.0` may also be present.

---

## GROUP 2 — Statin Therapy

### 2a. Active Prescriptions (MedicationRequest)

> Searches inline-coded MedicationRequests using RxNorm **ingredient-level** CUIs. Covers all 7 statin classes available in the US.

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|83367,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|36567,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|301542,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|42463,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|38413,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2103188&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### 2b. Reported Medications (MedicationStatement)

> Catches statin use documented via patient-reported intake, medication reconciliation, or care summary import — not tied to a prescription order.

```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|83367,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|36567,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|301542,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|42463,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|38413,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2103188&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:subject
```

| RxNorm CUI | Statin | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `83367` | Atorvastatin | Most prescribed; 40/80 mg = high-intensity |
| `36567` | Simvastatin | 40 mg max recommended due to myopathy risk |
| `301542` | Rosuvastatin | Preferred high-intensity; 20/40 mg |
| `42463` | Pravastatin | Moderate-intensity; common in elderly |
| `41493` | Lovastatin | Moderate-intensity; older agent |
| `38413` | Fluvastatin | Low/moderate-intensity |
| `2103188` | Pitavastatin | Low DDI profile; useful in special populations |

> ⚠️ **Overcounting caveat:** This query will return the entire statin-prescribed population (~30–40% of adults 40+). Without cross-referencing against LDL levels or FH diagnosis, the vast majority will be non-FH cardiovascular disease or primary prevention patients. **Use Group 1 and Group 3 to dedup.**

### 2c. PCSK9 Inhibitors (Highly FH-Specific — Bonus Query)

> Evolocumab and alirocumab are FDA-approved specifically for HeFH/HoFH and ASCVD. A patient on a PCSK9 inhibitor is a near-certain FH or very-high-risk ASCVD patient — a **precision enrichment signal** for your cascade screening cohort.

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1657013,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1654055&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

| RxNorm CUI | Drug | Indication |
|---|---|---|
| `1657013` | Evolocumab (Repatha) | HeFH, HoFH, ASCVD |
| `1654055` | Alirocumab (Praluent) | HeFH, ASCVD |

---

## GROUP 3 — Untreated/Undiagnosed Elevated LDL (Most Clinically Valuable)

> Uses the three primary LOINC codes for LDL — calculated (Friedewald), direct assay, and the legacy unspecified. Threshold of ≥190 mg/dL is the ACC/AHA guideline criterion for FH screening referral. `status=final,amended,corrected` excludes preliminary/cancelled results.

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2089-1,http://loinc.org|13457-7,http://loinc.org|18262-6&value-quantity=ge190|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mg/dL&status=final,amended,corrected&_include=Observation:subject
```

| LOINC | Description | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `2089-1` | LDL [Mass/vol] in Serum or Plasma | Legacy/general; widely used |
| `13457-7` | LDL by Friedewald calculation | Most common outpatient lab method |
| `18262-6` | LDL by direct assay | Used when TG > 400; hospital labs |

> ⚠️ **Statin-treatment confound:** Patients with FH already on statins may have a current LDL <190. This query is optimized to catch **undiagnosed, untreated** FH. Consider supplementing with a historical `date=le[statin start date]` filter per patient if refining further.

> ⚠️ **Unit note:** All US labs report in mg/dL. If your server holds mmol/L values, add a parallel query: `value-quantity=ge4.92|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mmol/L` (190 mg/dL ≈ 4.91 mmol/L).

---

## Post-Processing: Building the Union Cohort

```
Patient IDs from Group 1  →  High-specificity confirmed FH
        ∪
Patient IDs from Group 2c →  PCSK9 prescriptions (near-certain FH or severe ASCVD)
        ∪
Patient IDs from Group 3  →  Undiagnosed FH candidates (priority for genetic testing referral)
        ∪
Patient IDs from Group 2a/2b (filtered by Group 3 overlap) → Statin + high LDL = likely FH
```

**Prioritization for genetic testing/cascade screening:**
1. **Tier 1:** Group 1 (confirmed FH dx) + Group 2c (PCSK9 users) — refer directly
2. **Tier 2:** Group 3 ∩ Group 2a/2b (LDL ≥190 **and** on a statin) — high pre-test probability
3. **Tier 3:** Group 3 only (LDL ≥190, no statin, no FH code) — undiagnosed, screen urgently
4. **Deprioritize:** Group 2a/2b only (statin, no elevated LDL, no FH dx) — low FH probability

---

### 🔬 GROUP 1 — FH Diagnoses (Condition)

**1a. ICD-10-CM — Primary FH code + broad unspecified parent**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E78.01,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E78.00&_include=Condition:subject
```

**1b. SNOMED CT — FH concept hierarchy (parent, HeFH, HoFH, alternative parent)**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|13454006,http://snomed.info/sct|315497003,http://snomed.info/sct|398036000,http://snomed.info/sct|267432004&_include=Condition:subject
```

#### Code Reference — Group 1

| Code | System | Description | FH Relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| `E78.01` | ICD-10-CM | Familial hypercholesterolemia | **Primary FH code** — Het & Homo often coded identically |
| `E78.00` | ICD-10-CM | Pure hypercholesterolemia, unspecified | Broad parent — catches miscoded/pre-specific FH |
| `13454006` | SNOMED CT | Familial hypercholesterolaemia (disorder) | SNOMED parent concept for all FH |
| `315497003` | SNOMED CT | Heterozygous familial hypercholesterolaemia | HeFH — most prevalent form (~1:250) |
| `398036000` | SNOMED CT | Homozygous familial hypercholesterolaemia | HoFH — rare (~1:300,000), most severe |
| `267432004` | SNOMED CT | Familial pure hypercholesterolaemia | Legacy/alternative SNOMED mapping |

> ⚠️ **Note:** ICD-10-CM does not distinguish HeFH vs HoFH — both typically use `E78.01`. SNOMED CT subsumption (`?code:below=13454006`) may also be supported by your terminology server, which would capture all descendant FH concepts in a single query.

---

### 💊 GROUP 2 — Statin Therapy (MedicationRequest + MedicationStatement)

**2a. Active statin orders — MedicationRequest (all 7 commercially available statins)**
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|83367,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|301542,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|36567,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|42463,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41127,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|861634&status=active&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

**2b. Active statin use — MedicationStatement (patient-reported / reconciled meds)**
```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|83367,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|301542,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|36567,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|42463,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41127,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|861634&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:subject
```

#### Code Reference — Group 2

| RxNorm | Statin (Ingredient) | Key FH Dosing Context |
|---|---|---|
| `83367` | Atorvastatin | First-line for FH; high-intensity (40–80 mg) |
| `301542` | Rosuvastatin | High-intensity (20–40 mg); preferred for HoFH |
| `36567` | Simvastatin | Common; 40 mg cap limits efficacy in FH |
| `42463` | Pravastatin | Moderate-intensity; CYP2C9-independent |
| `41493` | Lovastatin | Moderate-intensity; older agent |
| `41127` | Fluvastatin | Low–moderate intensity |
| `861634` | Pitavastatin | Low–moderate; minimal drug–drug interaction |

> ⚠️ **Significant overcounting expected.** ~25–35% of adults 40–75 are statin-eligible for primary prevention — only a small fraction have FH. Post-query filtering strategy: cross-reference with Query Groups 1 and 3, then apply a clinical decision score (e.g., Dutch Lipid Clinic Network ≥6, MEDPED, Simon Broome) to triage.

> 📝 **High-intensity statin flag:** Patients on atorvastatin ≥40 mg or rosuvastatin ≥20 mg with LDL still ≥160 mg/dL despite treatment should be prioritized — this pattern is highly suggestive of FH.

### 📊 GROUP 3 — Elevated LDL Cholesterol (Observation)

**3a. LDL ≥190 mg/dL — all LDL measurement methods, final results**
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2089-1,http://loinc.org|13457-7,http://loinc.org|18262-6,http://loinc.org|22748-8,http://loinc.org|39469-2&value-quantity=ge190|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mg%2FdL&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

**3b. Optional — Extreme elevation flag (LDL ≥250 mg/dL, strong HoFH/HeFH signal)**
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2089-1,http://loinc.org|13457-7,http://loinc.org|18262-6,http://loinc.org|22748-8,http://loinc.org|39469-2&value-quantity=ge250|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mg%2FdL&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

#### Code Reference — Group 3

| LOINC | Description | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `2089-1` | LDL Cholesterol [Mass/vol] in Serum/Plasma | General/unspecified method — most common |
| `13457-7` | LDL Cholesterol — Friedewald equation | Calculated; inaccurate if TG >400 mg/dL |
| `18262-6` | LDL Cholesterol — Direct assay | Preferred in hypertriglyceridemia |
| `22748-8` | LDL Cholesterol — Direct method | Alternative direct assay LOINC |
| `39469-2` | LDL Cholesterol — Calculated | Some labs use this variant |

> ✅ **Highest clinical value for genetic testing referral.** An untreated LDL ≥190 mg/dL in an adult is diagnostic criteria for FH in multiple scoring systems. LDL ≥330 mg/dL suggests HoFH. If patients in Group 3 are **not** in Group 1, they represent **undiagnosed FH** — the highest-yield population for genetic testing and cascade screening of first-degree relatives.

### 🔀 Post-Query Union & Stratification Strategy

```
Population A  =  Group 1 patients               → Diagnosed FH (genetic testing to confirm subtype)
Population B  =  Group 2 MINUS Group 1           → Statin patients without FH diagnosis (low yield without further filter)
Population C  =  Group 3 MINUS Group 1           → Elevated LDL, undiagnosed (HIGHEST priority for new FH workup)
Population D  =  Group 2 ∩ Group 3 MINUS Group 1 → On statin AND high LDL AND no diagnosis (very high FH probability)

Final FH Candidate List = A ∪ C ∪ D
```

> 💡 **Cascade screening trigger:** For every confirmed FH patient in Population A or C, first-degree relatives (parents, siblings, children) have a 50% probability of carrying the same mutation. Consider querying `RelatedPerson` resources linked to confirmed FH patients or using FamilyMemberHistory to identify at-risk relatives:
```

FamilyMemberHistory?condition-code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E78.01
```
